title: RAG vs. wiki paradigm
body_markdown:
## RAG vs. wiki paradigm

Both retrieve context for an LLM. They differ on *where the work
happens*.

### RAG
Query time: embed the question, fetch the top-k chunks, stuff them
into the prompt. The corpus stays raw. Good for questions you haven't
anticipated; bad for synthesis that needs the full graph.

### Wiki
Ingestion time: the LLM reads documents and writes a structured wiki.
Query time just reads the wiki. Good for recurring questions and
for a *single person* who benefits from the compounding artifact.

### The middle path
A wiki that still has the raw sources linked. Queries prefer the
wiki first, fall through to the raw layer when specificity is
needed. That's the shape en2karp is taking.
